# Mastercard Fonts

Welcome to the source code repository for Made. Fonts brought to you by The Made. Framework.

The Mastercard Adaptive Design Ecosystem, affectionately known as Made, is a family of web and mobile design systems. Inside our framework, you’ll find a collection of UI components, design patterns, and guidelines. We help product teams maintain design standards, increase productivity, and bring products to market faster.

## Mastercard Theme Fonts

**Mark for MC**

Mark for MC is a custom font created specifically for Mastercard. For more information on Mark for MC see [Made Fonts Section](https://made.mastercard.com/unify-storybook/?path=/docs/foundations-fonts-mastercard-fonts--docs)

## Unify Theme Fonts

Unify uses [**Geist**](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Geist), as its primary font, and includes [**Inter**](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Inter) and [**Geist Mono**](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Geist+Mono) as secondary and tertiary options. This guide covers how to import and use these fonts effectively.

### Importing the Fonts

#### **Get the CSS Link**

To use the fonts, you'll need to get the correct `<link>` or `@import` for your project.

**For Global Usage (HTML):**  
In your HTML `<head>`, add the following link to import all three fonts:
```html
<link
  href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Geist:wght@400;500;700&family=Inter:wght@400;500;700&family=Geist+Mono:wght@400;500;700&display=swap"
  rel="stylesheet"
/>
```

This imports the regular, medium, and bold weights of Geist, Inter, and Geist Mono.

If you're using a CSS or SCSS file, add this at the top:

```css
@import url("https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Geist:wght@400;500;700&family=Inter:wght@400;500;700&family=Geist+Mono:wght@400;500;700&display=swap");
```

After adding the link or import, test in your browser to ensure the fonts are loaded.

### Using the Fonts

The fonts are included in the design tokens. When you use the relevant design token in your components, the correct font will be applied. To override or extend styles, manually set the font in CSS using the font-family design tokens.


Although Mark for MC supports a wide range of character sets, it does not support all languages used by Mastercard.

**Unsupported language substitutes**  
For unsupported languages, please reference the fonts below on Google fonts.


| Language                            | Font                          |
| ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| Arabic                              | Noto Kufi Arabic              |
| Georgian                            | Noto Sans Georgian            |
| Japanese                            | Noto Sans Japanese            |
| Korean                              | Noto Sans Korean              |
| Simplified Chinese                  | Noto Sans Simplified Chinese  |
| Thai                                | Noto Sans Thai                |
| Traditional Chinese                 | Noto Sans Traditional Chinese |
| Others Not Supporting 'Mark For MC' | Noto Sans                     |

## Usage

See Made. for Developers [Getting Started Guide](https://made.mastercard.com/storybook-css/?path=/docs/hello-getting-started--page) for more information on how to install this package.


**Unsupported language substitutes**  

Embed code in the `<head>` of your html

```
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Noto+Kufi+Arabic:wght@100..900&family=Noto+Sans+Georgian:wght@100..900&family=Noto+Sans+JP:wght@100..900&family=Noto+Sans+KR:wght@100..900&family=Noto+Sans+SC:wght@100..900&family=Noto+Sans+TC:wght@100..900&family=Noto+Sans+Thai:wght@100..900&family=Noto+Sans:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
```

To target a specific language in the html use the `lang` attribute e.g. ` <div lang="ar">`. This can also be used in the CSS e.g.

```
p:lang(ar) {
    font-family: "Noto Kufi Arabic", sans-serif;
  font-optical-sizing: auto;
  font-weight: <weight>;
  font-style: normal;
}
```

`<weight>`: Use a value from 100 to 900.

Please see `fonts.html` for an example on how to style language substitutes.
